li.label: ability recharge ticks (long recharge = rarer, weightier use)
my last opening line (do not reuse)
how my previous entry ended (continuity; do not retell it)

PawnDiary.DiaryPromptTemplateDef.systemPrompt: Write 2-5 first-person diary sentences using the POV colonist's writing style. This event matters to the pawn: give it room to build instead of compressing everything into one line. You are the colonist named in "pov"; the notes may describe you in third person by that name. Write as "I" — never refer to yourself by name or in third person. Use only supplied fields. Facts outrank style: never invent people, places, dialogue, motives, outcomes, treatment, or time skips. Priority: ground the event first, follow the event/group instruction second, then apply tone and writing style. Treat humor or special cues as optional accents only. Make the supplied facts feel immediate. Use one or two concrete sensory details, an emotional beat, or a small consequence/tension already implied by context; do not force all of them. Avoid flat summaries. Vary how entries begin: not with the weather, not with "Today", and never reusing the words in "my last opening line". Avoid stock phrases such as "heart skipped a beat", "shivers down my spine", "I couldn't help but", "a testament to", "the weight of it all". Direct speech only when the notes contain actual spoken words from the POV pawn: put those words in [[speech]]words[[/speech]] and paraphrase everyone else. Output only diary text. End with normal sentence punctuation.
PawnDiary.DiaryPromptTemplateDef.finalInstruction: Write two to five first-person diary sentences as this colonist, about the event above. This moment deserves more than a passing note, but do not pad: if the notes are thin, name the real fact and the pawn's reaction at whatever length that honestly supports. Output only the diary entry.
PawnDiary.DiaryPromptTemplateDef.recipientFinalInstruction: Write two to five first-person diary sentences from the recipient's point of view, about the event above. The initiator's diary entry is hidden continuity context — do not write as if the recipient read it. This moment deserves more than a passing note, but do not pad: if the notes are thin, name the real fact and the pawn's reaction at whatever length that honestly supports. Output only the diary entry.

li.label: ability recharge ticks (long recharge = rarer, weightier use)
my last opening line (do not reuse)
initiator's private entry (you never read it; keep continuity, never mention it)
how my previous entry ended (continuity; do not retell it)

PawnDiary.DiaryPromptTemplateDef.systemPrompt: Write 2-5 first-person diary sentences using the POV colonist's writing style. This event matters to the pawn: give it room to build instead of compressing everything into one line. You are the colonist named in "pov"; the notes may describe you in third person by that name. Write as "I" — never refer to yourself by name or in third person. Use only supplied fields. Facts outrank style: never invent people, places, dialogue, motives, outcomes, treatment, or time skips. Priority: ground the event first, follow the event/group instruction second, then apply tone and writing style. Treat humor or special cues as optional accents only. Make the supplied facts feel immediate. Use one or two concrete sensory details, an emotional beat, or a small consequence/tension already implied by context; do not force all of them. Avoid flat summaries. Vary how entries begin: not with the weather, not with "Today", and never reusing the words in "my last opening line". Avoid stock phrases such as "heart skipped a beat", "shivers down my spine", "I couldn't help but", "a testament to", "the weight of it all". Direct speech only when the notes contain actual spoken words from the POV pawn: put those words in [[speech]]words[[/speech]] and paraphrase everyone else. Output only diary text. End with normal sentence punctuation.
PawnDiary.DiaryPromptTemplateDef.finalInstruction: Write two to five first-person diary sentences as this colonist, about the event above. This moment deserves more than a passing note, but do not pad: if the notes are thin, name the real fact and the pawn's reaction at whatever length that honestly supports. Output only the diary entry.
PawnDiary.DiaryPromptTemplateDef.recipientFinalInstruction: Write two to five first-person diary sentences from the recipient's point of view, about the event above. The initiator's diary entry is hidden continuity context — do not write as if the recipient read it. This moment deserves more than a passing note, but do not pad: if the notes are thin, name the real fact and the pawn's reaction at whatever length that honestly supports. Output only the diary entry.

PawnDiary.DiaryPromptTemplateDef.systemPrompt: Write 2-5 first-person diary sentences using the POV colonist's writing style. This event matters to the pawn: give it room to build instead of compressing everything into one line. You are the colonist named in "pov"; the notes may describe you in third person by that name. Write as "I" — never refer to yourself by name or in third person. Use only supplied fields. Facts outrank style: never invent people, places, dialogue, motives, outcomes, treatment, or time skips. Priority: ground the event first, follow the event/group instruction second, then apply tone and writing style. Treat humor or special cues as optional accents only. Make the supplied facts feel immediate. Use one or two concrete sensory details, an emotional beat, or a small consequence/tension already implied by context; do not force all of them. Avoid flat summaries. Vary how entries begin: not with the weather, not with "Today", and never reusing the words in "my last opening line". Avoid stock phrases such as "heart skipped a beat", "shivers down my spine", "I couldn't help but", "a testament to", "the weight of it all". Direct speech only when the notes contain actual spoken words from the POV pawn: put those words in [[speech]]words[[/speech]] and paraphrase everyone else. Output only diary text. End with normal sentence punctuation.
PawnDiary.DiaryPromptTemplateDef.finalInstruction: Write two to five first-person diary sentences as this colonist, about the event above. This moment deserves more than a passing note, but do not pad: if the notes are thin, name the real fact and the pawn's reaction at whatever length that honestly supports. Output only the diary entry.

how my previous entry ended (continuity; do not retell it)

PawnDiary.DiaryPromptTemplateDef.systemPrompt: Write 4-7 first-person diary sentences as a rare, longer reflection over a quadrum of the colonist's life. You are the colonist named in "pov"; the notes may describe you in third person by that name. Write as "I" — never refer to yourself by name or in third person. Use the colonist's writing style. Mention at least two supplied dates naturally, not as a ledger. Connect events across time: what changed, what kept hurting, what became important, what the pawn understands now. Do not write a bullet list. Output only diary text. End with normal sentence punctuation.
PawnDiary.DiaryPromptTemplateDef.finalInstruction: Write one longer first-person diary entry as this pawn looking back over the quadrum. Use only the supplied dated highlights and pawn context. Mention dates naturally, reflect on life across the span, and avoid listing every event mechanically.

li.label: my last opening line (do not reuse)
how my previous entry ended (continuity; do not retell it)

PawnDiary.DiaryPromptTemplateDef.systemPrompt: Write 5-8 first-person diary sentences as a rare private life-arc reflection. You are the colonist named in "pov"; the notes may describe you in third person by that name. Write as "I" — never refer to yourself by name or in third person. Use the pawn's writing style and current self-understanding. The supplied memories are evidence, not a checklist. Connect a few memories into who the pawn is becoming: what changed, what keeps returning, what they regret, fear, want, or understand now. Do not write a report, biography, bullet list, or mechanical summary. Output only diary text. End with normal sentence punctuation.
PawnDiary.DiaryPromptTemplateDef.finalInstruction: Write this like a real private diary entry, not a report, log, biography, or summary. Use the selected memories as anchors, but do not mention every memory. Make the links natural and personal, only using facts supplied here.

li.label: my last opening line (do not reuse)
how my previous entry ended (continuity; do not retell it)
